The recording .WMA was converted to .mp3.
The bird is calling for three nights near our camp situated at elevation of 596m. exact coordinates is as follows. N13.63449, E123.34477
Based on my photos, the species we saw exhibit the rufous phase though we cannot judge if male or female.
Habitat is situated in a slightly rolling terrain vegetated with regenerating natural stands, planted with Kupang, Narra, Mahogany.  Major ground cover is a mosaic of coffee, climbing bamboos and natural regenerants.

Location: Mabalacat City (near  Mabalacat), Pampanga, Central Luzon
Remarks: The bird (adult male based on the red throat) was seen first without giving any sound, in a relaxed perching position. It hopped to another branch, cocking its tail upright. This recorded call/song came immediately after a playback. The call/song was captured using an iPhone. The call/song came about at ground level, and bird position was at eye level. Bird was seen perched in dense understory. The area is combination of tall grass and scrubs with occasional trees. While the bird was making this call/song, another similar call/song was heard not far away.
